findings.summary: The planned renderer API for sending OMP stdin commands does not match Maestro's existing preload contract.
findings.evidence: The plan targets src/main/preload/index.ts and src/renderer/global.d.ts and uses window.maestro.sendAgentStdinCommand(...) (plan.md:706-730, 742-748, 766, 773). The current preload surface exposes process methods under window.maestro.process via createProcessApi() (src/main/preload/index.ts:56-67, src/main/preload/process.ts:135-158), and global.d.ts declares those methods under MaestroAPI.process (src/renderer/global.d.ts:257-262).
findings.impact: An implementation that follows the plan will modify the wrong API surface and land inconsistent renderer typings and call sites.
findings.recommended_fix: Move the new stdin command method into src/main/preload/process.ts, expose it as window.maestro.process.sendAgentStdinCommand(...), declare it inside MaestroAPI.process in src/renderer/global.d.ts, and update the plan examples and acceptance text to match.

findings.summary: Unit 2-C depends on renderer-visible init/system parser events that the current Maestro event pipeline does not emit.
findings.evidence: The plan expects useAgentListeners.ts to handle init ParsedEvents and system text 'OMP ready' (plan.md:758-760). ParsedEvent only exposes contextWindow inside usage, not as a top-level init property (src/main/parsers/agent-output-parser.ts:49-91). StdoutHandler emits usage, session-id, thinking-chunk, tool-execution, and final result data, but not generic init/system events to the renderer (src/main/process-manager/handlers/StdoutHandler.ts:297-309, 336, 397-401, 462-487).
findings.impact: The stated ready gate and context-window/session updates cannot be implemented as written; the plan either will not compile or will never drive the renderer state it describes.
findings.recommended_fix: Revise the phase to either add explicit main-to-renderer IPC events for OMP ready/init state or retarget the renderer integration to existing session-id and usage events, with any missing ready-state signal added explicitly.

findings.summary: The phase does not map per-tab abort and cleanup behavior to the actual renderer control points that currently own them.
findings.evidence: The plan says tab close should kill the OMP process for that tab and the abort button should send { type: 'abort' } (plan.md:676-678, 762-767). In the current code, tab close only performs renderer state updates through performTabClose -> closeTab (src/renderer/hooks/tabs/useTabHandlers.ts:629-666, src/renderer/utils/tabHelpers.ts:394-493). The active interrupt path always calls window.maestro.process.interrupt(targetSessionId) with fallback kill (src/renderer/hooks/agent/useInterruptHandler.ts:63-77, 229-231).
findings.impact: OMP tabs can leak persistent processes on close, and the existing abort button will continue to send SIGINT/kill semantics instead of the OMP RPC abort command.
findings.recommended_fix: Expand Phase 2 to explicitly cover the real control points: add per-tab process teardown to tab-close/session-close flows, update useInterruptHandler.ts and mirrored remote interrupt logic to branch OMP tabs to sendAgentStdinCommand({ type: 'abort' }), and specify how the correct per-tab process is identified.

findings.summary: Silently ignoring extension_ui_request may stall extension-driven OMP turns.
findings.evidence: The plan says Phase 2 ignores extension_ui_request (plan.md:974). In OMP RPC mode, dialog-style extension UI requests are stored in pendingRequests and emitted as extension_ui_request until the client responds (oh-my-pi packages/coding-agent/src/modes/rpc/rpc-mode.ts:78-118, 267-286).
findings.impact: Base prompt/response flows can still work, but extension-triggered interactions may appear hung if Maestro never answers the request.
findings.recommended_fix: Either constrain Phase 2 acceptance to non-extension scenarios or add a minimal response policy so blocking extension UI requests fail closed instead of waiting indefinitely.
